mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rate levels reflect weak aggregate demand and compressed household income, reducing expected corporate revenues and pushing equity discount rates higher via risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment breaches 9.0%, margin compression and corporate default risk escalate non-linearly, triggering risk-off repricing in equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns average positive when UNRATE is greater than 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A negative divergence between industrial production and retail sales—specifically month-over-month decline in INDPRO coinciding with month-over-month increase in RSAFS—signals a supply-constrained economy where input costs rise faster than final demand can absorb, compressing aggregate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of INDPRO declining while RSAFS rises does not produce negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the nominal federal funds rate is below 0.25%, a CPIAUCSL reading above its year-ago level signals negative real rates that incentivize risk-taking and corporate pricing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, a CPIAUCSL level above its year-ago reading does not coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over a 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 650 thousand units indicate a stalled residential construction sector that eliminates construction employment growth and durable goods demand, transmitting a negative demand shock to broad corporate revenues and pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when HOUST is below 650 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and rising unemployment create a negative feedback loop where falling output begets job losses that further depress demand; the joint deterioration predicts sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where INDPRO declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month, the average S&P 500 5-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls breaks the labor market recovery trend, triggering a sudden downward revision in aggregate demand expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS declines from the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of retail sales signal robust consumer demand that translates directly into revenue growth for listed corporations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS level exhibits a rank correlation below 0.02 with 60-day S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.
